title: Light-band route approach phase
summary: Lane 3 should support the next tactile route wave with one tiny Tundra or Coastal Scrub approach/readability seam instead of another destination push.

execution_order.why_now: A tiny spatial read can make the next route-feel proof more tactile without more density.
The next lane-3 move should support route feel, not open another landmark family.
The change should make approach feel better without looking like a new destination wave.

guardrails: No new signature pocket, lookout family, or major height spike.
Do not reopen the beach opener or treeline return family.
Keep the pass recoverable and low-density.

main_286_focus.target_family: snow-meadow drift hold into thaw-skirt entry
preferred_shape: one tiny approach or spacing pass inside the existing tundra route band
something the player feels in motion before they read about it
prefer slightly shortening the empty handoff from the drift hold into the first thaw-skirt step
avoid: stacking geometry into the existing bluff lookout band
changing route-board or notebook copy for this pass
main_286_focus.verification_note: Prefer deterministic tundra geometry/runtime coverage first; a fresh real-start Tundra browser pass is optional follow-on proof if the shared browser lane is clear.
